data.title: The dYdX community voted to increase the protocol fee buyback ratio to 75%.
data.description: Decentralized exchange dYdX announced on its X platform that, based on community voting results, it will increase the percentage of protocol fees used for buying back $DYDX tokens from 25% to 75%. This policy is effective today, meaning that 75% of the fees collected by the protocol will be directly used to buy back DYDX tokens on the open market.

data.title: Ethereum Leaders Launch ‘Trustless Manifesto’ to Protect Crypto Decentralization
data.description:
Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin and prominent developers released the Trustless Manifesto establishing strict decentralization criteria, following former developer revelations that five to ten insiders control protocol direction through funding and attention allocation.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/ethereum-leaders-launch-trustless-manifesto-to-protect-crypto-decentralization/

data.title: Investment app Public acquires Alto's crypto business for $65 million.
data.description: Investment app Public has acquired the cryptocurrency business of cryptocurrency IRA provider Alto for $65 million in cash and stock. Public will reportedly fully integrate Alto's cryptocurrency accounts by 2026, while Alto will continue to operate as a service provider. Alto will also continue to operate its other businesses independently, including private lending and venture capital investments. (Fortune)

data.title: Government Removing Barriers for Bitcoin and Crypto: US Treasury Secretary
data.description:
American Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has stated that the government is looking to remove regulatory barriers hampering the adoption of Bitcoin.
source: https://zycrypto.com/government-removing-barriers-for-bitcoin-and-crypto-us-treasury-secretary/

data.description:
XRP added $10 billion to its market capitalization in less than 24 hours, lifting its total market cap valuation to … Continue reading The post $10 billion floods into XRP as Nasdaq launches first U. S.
source: https://finbold.com/10-billion-floods-into-xrp-as-nasdaq-launches-first-u-s-spot-xrp-etf/

data.title: Uniswap Achieves Record Monthly Trading Volume in October 2025
data.description: According to PANews, Uniswap recorded its highest-ever monthly trading volume in October 2025, reaching approximately $116.6 billion. This marks a new milestone for the platform since its inception, as reported by Token Terminal data.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Experiences Decline Amid Tech Sector Developments
data.description: According to PANews, the U.S. stock market opened with declines on November 13, 2025. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell by 0.28%, the S&P 500 index decreased by 0.44%, and the Nasdaq Composite dropped by 0.67%. In the tech sector, Alibaba saw a rise of 3.75% as it prepares a significant overhaul of its AI applications to compete directly with ChatGPT. JD.com experienced a modest increase of 0.13% following its third-quarter revenue surpassing expectations. Cisco shares surged by 6.8% after the company raised its full-year profit and revenue forecasts.

data.title: Michael Saylor: Bitcoin's market capitalization will surpass that of gold by 2035
data.description: In an interview, Strategy founder Michael Saylor stated that Bitcoin's market capitalization will surpass that of gold by 2035. He said, "I have no doubt that by 2035, Bitcoin will become a larger asset class than gold." (Watcher Guru)

data.title: Bloomberg analysts: Canary XRP ETF saw $26 million in trading volume within 30 minutes of listing.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote on the X platform that the Canary XRP ETF “XRPC” saw a trading volume of $26 million in the first 30 minutes of its listing, far exceeding the previous estimate of $17 million, and is very likely to break the BSOL record of $57 million, potentially becoming the highest trading volume record for any crypto ETF on its first day of issuance this year.

data.description:
If financial nihilism has driven you into memecoins, zero-day options, and sports betting, consider financial optimism instead
source: https://blockworks.co/news/financial-optimism

data.title: Analysts: The Fed's anticipated hawkish repricing continues, potentially ending the US stock market's winning streak.
data.description: U.S. stocks opened lower, potentially ending a four-day winning streak, as investors grappled with the lack of U.S. economic data despite the end of the government shutdown. Carol Schleif, chief market strategist at BMO Private Wealth, said the government reopening was a "positive" for the market. "If the government resumes operations and economic data starts being released, we wouldn't be surprised by market volatility in the coming weeks." Thursday's decline came as traders focused on the December Federal Reserve meeting, reducing their bets on a rate cut. Collins indicated she prefers to "keep rates stable for a period" to balance inflation and employment risks. Bostic also stated that inflation remains a clearer risk to the U.S. economy. "With the end of the U.S. government shutdown and the continued hawkish repricing expected by the Fed, the market struggled to gain momentum this morning," said Karl Schamotta, chief market strategist at Corpay. (Jinshi)

data.title: Tether assisted Thai police and the U.S. Secret Service in tracking down and seizing 12 million USDT from a transnational fraud network.
data.description: Tether announced its support for a coordinated international operation by the Royal Thai Police and the U.S. Secret Service, which seized approximately 12 million USDT (worth about 400 million Thai baht) in connection with a large fraud network operating in Southeast Asia. The operation, conducted by the Technology Crime Suppression Bureau (TCSD) under Thailand's Ministry of Digital Economy and Society, is part of a broader effort to combat online fraud and money laundering. According to the Royal Thai Police, 73 suspects have been arrested, including 51 Thai citizens and 22 foreign nationals, and assets worth over 522 million Thai baht have been seized.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Maintains its price forecast for Bitcoin to reach $170,000 within 6-12 months.
data.description: JPMorgan analysts stated that Bitcoin's ever-increasing production costs (currently estimated at around $94,000) have historically acted as a price floor, meaning there is limited room for a decline from current levels. The analysts reiterated their forecast of a roughly $170,000 increase in Bitcoin's price over the next 6-12 months, noting that Bitcoin currently consumes approximately 1.8 times the risk capital required for gold. Based on this calculation, Bitcoin's market capitalization of approximately $2.1 trillion would need to increase by about 67%, implying a theoretical price close to $170,000. (The Block)

data.title: Magic Eden: 30% of secondary market proceeds will be used for a buyback program.
data.description: Magic Eden announced a buyback program on the X platform, stating that starting immediately, 30% of its secondary market revenue will be used for buybacks, with 15% allocated to on-chain ME token buybacks and the remaining 15% to NFT buybacks (with priority given to collectibles within the Solana ecosystem).

data.title: XRP ETF Makes Historic Wall Street Debut as Trading Begins on Nasdaq
data.description:
Key Takeaways: XRP’s first U. S. ETF (XRPC) goes live. The fund gives regulated spot exposure to XRP.
source: https://coindoo.com/xrp-etf-makes-historic-wall-street-debut-as-trading-begins-on-nasdaq/

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 103,000 USDT with a Narrowed 0.68% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Nov 13, 2025, 14:53 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 103,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 103,230.289063 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.68% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: A joint operation between Thailand and the FBI recovered $432,000 worth of cryptocurrency from a European hacker.
data.description: Thai officials announced Wednesday that they had successfully recovered over $432,000 (14 million baht) in stolen digital assets from an Eastern European cybercriminal hiding in Phuket. According to local media reports, Lieutenant General Surapong Premput, who leads the Thai Cybercrime Investigation Bureau (CCIB), revealed that "Operation 293" successfully seized and returned $320,000 worth of cryptocurrency to Thai victims. The hacker allegedly deployed malware to compromise victims' devices and steal authentication keys and mnemonic phrases, crucial access credentials for encrypted accounts. (Decrypt)

data.title: Zero-knowledge authentication startup Self has raised $9 million in seed funding, with participation from Greenfield Capital and others.
data.description: Zero-knowledge identity verification startup Self has raised $9 million in seed funding. The round was led by Greenfield Capital, Startup Capital Ventures x SBI Fund, Spearhead VC, Verda Ventures, Fireweed Ventures, and angel investors including Casey Neistat, Sreeram Kannan (EigenLayer), Sandeep Nailwal (Polygon), Julien Bouteloup (Curve), Jill Carlson (Espresso), and Hart Lambur (Across Protocol). Self provides privacy-preserving identity infrastructure that uses zero-knowledge proofs and verifiable credentials to verify attributes such as age or uniqueness without revealing personal data. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Decentralized privacy computing project Acurast raises $11 million in funding, with participation from Gavin Wood and others.
data.description: Decentralized privacy computing project Acurast announced the completion of a $11 million funding round, with participation from Ethereum co-founder and Polkadot founder Gavin Wood, MN Capital founder Michael Van The Poppe, and GlueNet founder Ogle, among others. The project plans to launch its mainnet on November 17th and is reportedly capable of tamper-proof execution and secure hardware verification on consumer-grade mobile phones. (Cointelegraph)
